title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Tyler Samaniego gave up four runs in the first inning, resulting in a 4-3 loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts and no walks.
The Red Sox are undecided on Brayan Bello's role after his success in relief, despite struggles as a starting pitcher.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Boston Celtics fans and the TD Garden atmosphere during a recent livestream.
Boston is among the contenders for Antetokounmpo, but the Miami Heat currently lead in trade talks.
Speculation continues on whether Boston will trade Jaylen Brown for Antetokounmpo in a potential multi-team deal.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police ended Harvard's Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd and safety concerns.
Massachusetts State Police responded at 5 a.m. to move a large group attempting illegal swimming in the Charles River.
Cambridge and Harvard University Police assisted; no immediate reports of arrests or injuries were confirmed.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is available on Peacock, featuring Uma Thurman's renowned performance in Quentin Tarantino's epic.
'Spider-Noir' starring Nicolas Cage is streaming on Prime Video, offering a choice between color or black & white viewing styles.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2 is on Netflix with Tina Fey and Will Forte returning in this comedy-drama adaptation.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: ESPN's Kiley McDaniel predicts Red Sox will draft Bo Lowrance, an 18-year-old third baseman, in 2026 MLB Draft.
Lowrance, from South Carolina, is a 6-foot-5 high school prospect committed to the University of Virginia.
Scouts compare Lowrance to Freddie Freeman; MLB.com praises his hitting but notes need for strength adjustments.

title: Lowell father and daughter charged in alleged payroll tax scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lim Ou and Nalen Ou from Lowell charged with federal tax crimes for failing to report $48 million in cash payroll.
The Ous allegedly ran staffing firms using check-cashing businesses to covertly pay workers and avoid tax obligations.
The criminal charges could result in up to five-year prison sentences and significant financial penalties for the pair.

title: Report: Christian Gonzalez unlikely to participate in OTAs under current Patriots contract
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez is not participating in Patriots OTAs due to a contract dispute, seeking a substantial pay raise.
Gonzalez aims to earn $32-35 million annually, compared to his current $4 million rookie contract.
Patriots cleared cap space by restructuring Mike Onwenu's contract, potentially aiding Gonzalez's extension negotiations.

title: Search underway for driver who led cops on a chase through Everett, Malden, Stoneham
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A suspect involved in a drug investigation led a police chase through Everett, Malden, and Stoneham, Massachusetts, Friday morning.
The suspect's vehicle struck a State Police cruiser and nearly hit a Malden officer before being abandoned in Stoneham.
Authorities are searching for the driver and a passenger who fled on foot; the suspect had multiple outstanding warrants.

title: Ex-Patriots HC Jerod Mayo has a new job away from football
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jerod Mayo, former Patriots head coach, became managing director at Fifth Down Capital in February.
Mayo was fired after a 4-13 season with the Patriots in 2024, leading to the team's No. 4 draft pick.
Fifth Down Capital is a private equity firm based in Needham, investing in technology, healthcare, and retail sectors.

title: Mass. man reports beating Mount Everest speed climbing record
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Andrews from Massachusetts claims to have broken the Everest speed climbing record with supplemental oxygen, reaching the summit in 9 hours, 55 minutes.
Nepali climber Lhakpa Gelu Sherpa held the previous record of 10 hours, 56 minutes since 2003; Andrews's time awaits official verification.
Andrews is an ultra-runner and high-altitude speed climber known for over 100 world records, dedicating the climb to late friend Greg Krupa.
